description_long: This object is a butterfly knife, also known as a balisong, featuring a sleek, double-edged straight blade with a matte black finish. The blade is flanked by two symmetrical handles, which are metallic and display a marbled yellow-gold coloration, likely anodized or coated for visual appeal. The handles are split and joined by pivot points near the blade, allowing them to rotate and enclose the blade when closed. A latch mechanism is visible at the base of the handles, used to secure the knife in its open or closed position. The overall construction appears robust, with visible screws and hardware reinforcing the pivot and handle structure. The knife's design is both functional and visually striking, suitable for utility or display in an urban environment.
description: A butterfly knife with a matte black double-edged blade and marbled yellow-gold metallic handles, featuring a pivoting mechanism and latch.
description_view_0: The knife is viewed edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the closed alignment of the handles.
description_view_1: The knife is seen from the front, displaying the full length of the black blade and the marbled yellow-gold handles with the latch visible at the base.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, highlighting the slimness of the blade and the parallel handles.
description_view_3: A rear view of the knife, showing the back of the blade and the handles, with the latch mechanism more prominent.
attribute_handle_design: split, metallic, marbled yellow-gold, straight with latch and pivot hardware
